summary: Vercel dependency-conflict fix verified. date-fns downgraded to 3.6.0, .npmrc with legacy-peer-deps=true present. `npm install --dry-run` completes cleanly (exit 0, zero ERESOLVE errors). Frontend runtime fully regressed: home page, brand, hero, 5 collection cards, 5 showroom gallery blocks, rate ticker, WhatsApp FAB, and contact form submission all working with zero console errors.

primary_success_criterion.result: PASS - Vercel build simulation will succeed

verified_checks: npm install --dry-run: exit 0, no ERESOLVE, no 'unable to resolve dependency tree'
Navbar brand 'Abhushan Nirmata' visible (verified via screenshot)
5 collection cards render (gold, bridal, diamond, silver, one-gram-gold)
5 showroom gallery blocks render (selectors are data-testid=gallery-block-*, not showroom-block-* as stated in request)
Rate ticker displays: Gold 24K Rs 12,807/g, Gold 22K Rs 11,740/g, Silver Rs 192/g (GET /api/rates working)
Contact form submit -> success toast 'Enquiry sent. We will get back to you shortly.'
Zero console errors related to date-fns / react-day-picker / calendar
Zero console errors overall on home page load
critical_code_review_comments: Non-blocking: Review request referenced data-testid='showroom-block-*' but implementation uses 'gallery-block-*'. Consider aligning naming for future contracts, but no functional impact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Vercel ERESOLVE fix (date-fns 4.1.0 -> 3.6.0 + .npmrc legacy-peer-deps=true) is fully verified. `npm install --dry-run` passes cleanly, frontend runtime intact. No further action needed on this fix.
